text:
There once was a garden that bloomed with vibrant flowers. Each flower was unique; roses with stripes, tulips with sparkling eyes. The roses held tiny lanterns at their buds.

Every evening after sunset, the roses would dance around, their lanterns casting soft light. The tulips clapped their petals together in laughter. Sunflowers turned their faces towards the moon.

One evening, a butterfly landed on a pansy. It fluttered its wings and sang a song of magic. The pansy sprouted new petals frilly enough to hold tiny glasses.

The garden was enchanted with this magic.

The next morning, the pansy had an idea. It gathered its new friends — the stripes roses, laughter-tasting tulips, and dream-filled moonflowers.

"Friends," it sang, "let us host a festivity!"

They crafted tiny glasses from petals, filled them with sparkling dreams, and drank them under the silver moonlight.

They danced until dawn. When the sun broke through
